meta_title: For Students: API integration guide to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: API Integration Guide to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Learn to master API integrations and overcome debugging hurdles. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first SaaS app.
content.answer_block: Debugging API integrations is best solved by isolating the request-response cycle. Use Claude Code to audit your network calls and Antigravity to generate robust error-handling wrappers. By validating payloads before transmission, you transform cryptic stack traces into actionable logs, drastically reducing your development cycle and building professional-grade, resilient software.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and your console is bleeding red error messages. You’ve spent hours trying to connect your first API, but the handshake fails, the data is malformed, and the documentation feels like a different language. This is the 'debugging wall'—the exact moment most students quit. But it doesn't have to be the end of your project.

Today, we shift from guessing to engineering. By leveraging modern AI agents like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can treat your code editor as a pair-programming partner. We will replace frustration with a systematic workflow that turns every API integration into a successful, portfolio-ready feature.

steps.title: Define the API Contract
steps.description: Before writing code, define the expected schema to avoid runtime surprises. Use AI to parse docs into a clean interface.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this API documentation [URL] and generate a TypeScript interface for the primary endpoint request and response payloads.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Create a schema.ts file based on the API requirements provided.'

steps.title: Isolate the Request Logic
steps.description: Create a standalone utility file to handle the API connection. This prevents mixing business logic with network calls.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust API client using fetch or axios with built-in error handling and status code logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit lib/api-client.ts 'Implement a wrapper that logs every request and response to the console.'

steps.title: Inject Mock Data for Testing
steps.description: Test your logic without hitting real endpoints to avoid rate limits and save development time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock data generator that matches the interface from step 1 for testing my UI components.

steps.title: Automate Error Interception
steps.description: Implement a global interceptor to catch API failures before they crash your frontend.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that catches 4xx and 5xx errors and returns a user-friendly message object.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit lib/api-client.ts 'Add a response interceptor to handle common API error codes gracefully.'

steps.title: Verify and Deploy
steps.description: Conduct a final audit of your integration and prepare for production deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my API integration code for security best practices and performance bottlenecks.

pro_tips: Always log the raw request body before it leaves the browser; 90% of bugs are just malformed JSON headers.
Use 'Environment Variables' for all API keys, even in local development, to build the habit of security early.
If an API returns a 403, check your headers first—usually, the content-type is missing or incorrectly set.
Treat your API client as a 'Service Layer'; never call APIs directly inside your UI components.
Master the 'Network' tab in your browser's DevTools; it is the single most important tool for debugging API failures.
content.next_steps: Once your API integration is live, focus on building a unit test suite for your service layer to ensure your app stays stable as you add new features.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a developer with a repeatable process for solving complex problems. Your portfolio is growing, and your confidence is unmatched. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
